titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 1330

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=156 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 20:44:43.369013+00 | translation moa=157 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 20:44:43.378593+00

p.cl:
. Ricardus dei gratia episcopus sancti Andree . et Edelred abbas Rieuallensis . et Willelmus abbas . Mailrosensis  Vniuersis sancte Matris
ecclesie filiis . salutem .

nos mandato Alexandri dei gratia summi pontificis apud
berewic terminasse controuersiam que fuit inter ecclesiam dunolmensem et ecclesiam de Kelchou super capella
de Ercheldun secundum formam literarum domini pape  que talis est . scilicet quod si prior dunolmensis quadra
genariam posset probare prescripcionem sine interruptione super capella de Ercheldun  ut monachis ecclesie de
Kelchou super capella prenominata silencium omni appelatione et excusatione postposita imponeremus .

Prescriptione
itaque quadragenaria in nostra presencia iuramento . x . ii . sacerdotum . et unius diaconi et duorum laicorum canonice
probata  predictis monachis de capella prenominata silencium indiximus . et eam . ecclesie dunolmensi inperpetuum adiu
dicauimus .

testibus . Osberto abbate de geddewrthe . Nicolao cancellario . Andrea Archidiacono laudonie . Aiulfo et Iocio de
canis . Roberto canonico de sancto Andrea . Magistro herberto . Elia de ildertun . herueio et Eilaf capellanis de berewic .
Romfar capellano . Alano presbitero de Hiltune . Alano presbitero de Walesede . Magistro Gocelino .
Richard, by the grace of God bishop of St Andrews, Æthelred abbot of Rievaulx, and William abbot of Melrose, to all sons of holy Mother Church send greeting.

that at Berwick, by the command of Alexander, by the grace of God supreme pontiff, we concluded the dispute which there was between the church of Durham and the church of Kelso over the chapel of Earlston according to the tenor of the letters of the lord pope, which is such, that is to say, that if the prior of Durham was able to prove forty-year title without interruption in respect of the chapel of Earlston, that we would impose on the monks of the church of Kelso in respect of the aforenamed chapel restraint from all appeal and the laying-aside of accusation.

The forty-year title having been proved, therefore, by canonical oath-swearing, in our presence, of 12 priests, one deacon, and two laymen, we have imposed restraint on the aforesaid monks concerning the aforenamed chapel, and adjudged it to the church of Durham in perpetuity.

As witness Osbert the abbot of Jedburgh, Nicholas the chancellor, Andrew the archdeacon of Lothian, Aiulf and Joce the deans, Robert the canon of St Andrews, Master Herbert, Elias of Ilderton, Hervey and Eilaf the chaplains of Berwick, Romfar the chaplain, Alan the priest of Hilton, Alan the priest of Wallsend, Master Gocelin.
